concept: Moment / Brief Point in Time / Present Instant
description: A very brief period of time; a particular point in time. 'Shemu' names the smallest meaningful unit of experienced time—the now that is always passing, the point where life actually happens.
sound_symbolism: The word arrives and passes. 'She' /ʃe/ begins with soft breath, presence forming. Then 'mu' grounds briefly, the moment held before releasing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'ha shemu' (this moment). Related to 'tiroe' (instant) but emphasizes the experiential quality rather than pure brevity.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The moment is all we have—past is memory, future is imagination, only this moment is real.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Moments of attention determined survival—the instant of noticing, the moment of action.
